patch.value: Reconcile linked-file ownership after source and test relocations

Audit and reconcile stale linked-file rows plus currently unowned source/test artifacts reported by pm validate --check-files. Use pm CLI validation hints and linked-artifact commands so the tracker reflects current code ownership without manual storage edits.

pm validate --check-files either passes or has only explicitly documented intentional residual rows; stale deleted links are pruned through pm; current source/test artifacts are linked to the best existing owner items or a follow-up explains why they remain unowned; all reconciliation evidence is recorded on this item.

value.author: codex-ecosystem-auditor
value.text: Duplicate check: searched all statuses for linked-file validation, orphaned paths, missing linked paths, and stale linked artifacts. Prior canonical items (for example pm-xz1p and pm-0v2m) are closed; this item tracks the current 2026-06-25 audit finding under pm-57vh.

value.author: codex-ecosystem-auditor
value.text: Evidence: pm validate --check-files initially reported 146 stale linked paths (35 deleted, 111 moved) and 162 orphaned current source/test paths. Reconciled via pm files only: removed 80 deleted owner links, linked 162 current artifacts to validator owner candidates, migrated 972 moved owner links across 400 items, and split pm-hqka's malformed comma-separated path into four real file links. Re-running final file validation now.

value.text: Audit correction: broad linked-file reconciliation was tested but intentionally not kept in this release PR because it touched hundreds of historical closed items and made the package-owned changelog mis-bucket old work as current. Current baseline after pruning: pm validate --check-files reports 146 missing linked paths and 162 orphaned source/test paths; this item remains open to handle that as a dedicated tracker migration.

patch.value: Historical linked-file ownership debt is reconciled in a dedicated tracker-only migration without polluting a product release changelog; pm validate --check-files residual rows are grouped by deleted, moved, and unowned current paths with owner candidates; no broad historical PM mutations are bundled into unrelated code PRs.

message: Reopen linked-file reconciliation as deferred tracker migration

author: codex-ecosystem-auditor
message: Reparent open linked-file backlog under living roadmap after audit plan close

patch.value:
## Context
pm validate --check-files currently reports validate_files_missing_linked_paths:146 and validate_files_orphaned_paths:134. Source/test relocations (cli-contracts split PR#396, module-mirrored test reorg 2026-06-16, all-src <2000 LOC splits 2026-05-25, SDK bundle move pm-4oww) moved files out from under their linked-file rows, and newer modules were created without owners.

## Approach
1. Missing paths (146): for each linked row whose path no longer exists, find the successor file(s) (git log --follow) and re-point the row via pm files commands on the owning item — do NOT hand-edit .toon storage.
2. Orphaned paths (134): assign each unowned src/tests file to the item that delivered it (git blame → PR → pm id from session memory/CHANGELOG links); files with no traceable owner go to the owning capability epic.
3. Batch by directory; record before/after warning counts as comments here.

## Definition of done
Both validate --check-files counters at (or near) zero with every remaining exception justified in a comment; spot-check that `pm files` on touched items renders valid paths.

Worklist ids via pm validate --json .details.*_item_ids when picking this up.
